title: Overview Codex Version Inventory
scope: Detect every Codex CLI executable visible to the current user.
Show which Codex executable is active for the current VS Code/user environment.
Explain version conflicts between user-level, system-level, workspace-local, and extension-bundled Codex binaries.
Surface upgrade guidance without mutating auth or installing packages automatically.
non_goals: Do not auto-upgrade Codex from the Overview page.
Do not use sudo or modify /usr/bin/codex.
Do not hide older Codex versions; older versions are useful evidence.
Do not treat global npm, system package, and extension-bundled Codex as the same installation.

tasks.title: Detect active Codex executable
tasks.details: Resolve the active codex path using the same PATH/environment CMA uses for managed commands, then run `codex --version` and record path, version, and source.

tasks.title: Inventory all visible Codex versions
tasks.details: Collect `type -a codex`-style paths, common system/user paths, workspace node_modules installs, and VS Code bundled Codex paths; dedupe by realpath.

tasks.title: Classify Codex install sources
tasks.details: Label each executable as user npm global, system npm global, workspace local, VS Code bundled, wrapper, symlink, or unknown.

tasks.title: Render Overview version card
tasks.details: Add an Overview card showing active path/version, all discovered versions, and warnings when the active user version differs from system or bundled versions.

tasks.title: Show safe upgrade guidance
tasks.details: Display copyable user-level commands such as `npm config set prefix ~/.local`, `npm install -g @openai/codex@latest`, `hash -r`, and `codex --version`.

tasks.title: Add version inventory tests
tasks.details: Test path dedupe, active path selection, mixed 0.118/0.128 version rendering, and neutral handling when a path cannot execute.

implementation_notes.summary: Added Node backend helpers and route `/api/codex/active` to resolve `codex` path via `which`/`where`, run `codex --version` in process env, and return structured active-Codex metadata.

Added Node backend endpoint `/api/codex/inventory` and helpers to enumerate PATH/known Codex executable candidates, dedupe by realpath, probe `--version` for each, and return structured inventory items.

Added install source classification to each Codex inventory item (`installSource` + `installSourceTags`) with symlink wrapper/system/user/workspace/bundled/unknown heuristics and preserved existing inventory structure.

Added dashboard state wiring for Codex inventory (`/api/codex/inventory`) into `fetchDashboardState` and rendered an Overview summary card from `activeCodex` + `codexInventory` with discovered versions and PATH/conflict copy.
Added explicit safe upgrade guidance lines in the existing Overview Codex summary card with copyable user-level commands and terminal profile note, without automating any changes.
Added backend regression test for Codex inventory mixed active/stale versions and non-executable path behavior via `readCodexInventory` with a deterministic `spawnSync` stub.

acceptance: Overview shows the active Codex path and version for the current user.
Overview shows all discovered Codex executables and their versions when executable.
The UI clearly distinguishes user-level `/home/clashuser/.local/bin/codex` from system-level `/usr/bin/codex` and VS Code bundled Codex.
A stale system Codex version does not appear as the active version when the user PATH activates a newer user-level Codex.
The UI provides safe upgrade guidance without running sudo or modifying auth files.
